summary: Iteration 3 (Care+ family co-care + Send test check-in) end-to-end tested. Backend: 42/42 pytest tests pass (30 regression + 12 new). New coverage: POST /messages/send-test writes message_logs and returns Twilio status (sent/failed/simulated); GET/POST /circle & /circle/invite for Care+ owners (role, plan, max_members=3, dedupe, own-email-block, cancel-invite); Basic owner invite blocked with 403 upgrade message; register-time auto-link (invited email -> household_owner_id set, onboarding_complete=true, member sees OWNER's parents/schedules via scope()); member blocked from POST /payment/checkout and POST /circle/invite with 403; owner can DELETE /circle/member/{id} and the removed user's own scope is empty. Frontend: full flow verified via Playwright — Owner signup -> Onboarding steps 0..4 (child/parent/plan-care_plus/schedule/activate) -> Dashboard; Parents tab Send button opens SendTestDialog (send-test-category select + send-test-confirm) and closes cleanly; Care circle tab (tab-circle) shows invite-form for Care+ owner, adds a pending invite (member email listed with '1/3 members used'); member signs up with invited email and is redirected DIRECTLY to /dashboard (bypasses onboarding, toast '💛 joined family care circle'), sees owner's parent 'TEST_UI_Amma' in Parents tab, and CircleTab shows 'Shared care circle · You're co-caring in TEST_UI_Owner's circle (owner@…)' with NO invite-form.

ui_bugs.issue: Member sees 'Finish setup' button because activation.whatsapp_activated is checked against the MEMBER's own activation_state (which is always false — members share data via scope but activation_state is user-scoped). Clicking it takes the member to /onboarding, which the member should never see (they were auto-linked). Fix: hide 'Finish setup' when user.household_owner_id is set, or check owner's activation_state, or route members away from /onboarding.
'Care circle' stat card shows 'Off' for a linked member even though the owner has activated WhatsApp — same root cause as above (member reads own activation_state, not owner's). Cosmetic.

action_items: In Dashboard.js, gate the 'Finish setup' button on !user.household_owner_id (member should never be pushed to onboarding). Ideally also fetch/derive owner's activation state so the 'Care circle' stat card shows 'Active' for shared members.
Consider guarding /onboarding route so that a user with household_owner_id gets redirected to /dashboard (belt-and-suspenders in case a member navigates manually).

Backend server.py POST /activation/activate uses str(user['_id']) for parents/schedules lookup instead of scope(user); a linked member calling it would get 400 'Please add a parent and a schedule'. Acceptable today (member has no need to activate) but be consistent with scope() — either 403 members explicitly or use scope().
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /activation/activate (lines 310-347) queries db.parents/db.schedules by str(user['_id']) but everywhere else uses scope(user). Recommend either scope() or an explicit is_member() 403 for consistency.

server.py /circle/accept is dead code paths-wise since register auto-links on email match; keeping it is fine, but there is no /accept UI. Consider removing or wire a 'Join family' page for users who register BEFORE the invite (they'd need to trigger accept manually).
circle_invites has no unique index on (owner_id, email, status) — duplicate detection uses find_one, which is fine, but a partial index would be safer under concurrency.

Dashboard.js SendTestDialog only lists categories.map without grouping; for Basic plans the 'medicine' etc reminder categories are available in the send-test picker (which is fine — this is an on-demand ping, not a scheduled message).
Dashboard.js CircleTab 'Finish setup' issue described above — member UX is not fully household-aware.
Twilio auth token is committed in backend/.env; rotate after testing (existing comment from prior iteration still valid).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_circle_and_send.py (new — 12 tests for send-test, care circle invite/accept/remove, basic-plan 403, auto-link, member data sharing)

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the requested iteration-3 flows (send-test, invite, auto-link, shared parents, member UI). 1 minor UX gap noted (Finish setup button for members).

test_credentials: Admin: admin@ayana.care / AyanaAdmin@2026. UI flow created: owner_ui_<hash>@example.com / test1234 (Care+, activated) + member_ui_<hash>@example.com / test1234 (auto-linked). Pre-existing: owner1@example.com / test1234 (Care+) and sib1@example.com (linked member) per review context.
seed_data_creation: Pytest run created ~15 TEST_*/c_*/sib_*/dup_*/cancel_*/member_*/toremove_*@example.com users + one TEST_UI_Owner + TEST_UI_Member via UI. No cleanup performed; identifiable by TEST_/e2e_/owner_ui_/member_ui_ prefixes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests split across /app/backend/tests/test_ayana_api.py (regression, 30 tests) and /app/backend/tests/test_circle_and_send.py (iteration 3, 12 tests). Total 42 tests, xdist=2, ~13s. Onboarding testids are step0-next/step1-next/step3-next/step4-activate (NOT child-next/parent-next). PhoneInput testid pattern: fill '<testid>' with digits only — country code selector is '<testid>-code'. WHATSAPP_ENABLED=true — send-test/activation return 'failed' for non-sandbox numbers, not a bug. The one open UX bug is the 'Finish setup' button + 'Care circle: Off' stat leaking through for members because activation_state is user-scoped while parents/schedules are household-scoped.
rca of the issue: Member 'Finish setup' UX bug — RCA: In server.py, /activation is fetched with str(user['_id']) and the record is written per-user during owner's activation. When a member logs in, GET /activation returns {} (default) so activation.whatsapp_activated=false, and Dashboard.js renders the Finish setup CTA (line 81-83). Fix in frontend: change condition to `!activation.whatsapp_activated && !user?.household_owner_id`. Optionally also change backend to return the owner's activation for members: `state = await db.activation_state.find_one({'user_id': scope(user)})`.
